Interface ASTIteratorListener

All Known Implementing Classes:
ASTIteratorAdapter
public interface ASTIteratorListener
A listener for AST traversal events signaled by ASTIterators.

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    static final int
    ENTER_ALL
    Return value for ASTListener methods, that signals that all children should be entered without further questions.
    static final int
    ENTER_NONE
    Return value for ASTListener methods, that signals that no child node should be entered.
    static final int
    ENTER_SOME
    Return value for ASTListener methods, that signals that only some children nodes should be entered - each child will be queried separately.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    boolean
    enterChildNode(ASTIterator it, NonTerminalProgramElement thisNode, ProgramElement childNode)
    Determines whether or not a given child node should be visited.
    int
    enterChildren(ASTIterator it, NonTerminalProgramElement thisNode)
    Called to determine whether or not children nodes should be visited.
    void
    enteringNode(ASTIterator it, ProgramElement node)
    This method is called whenever an AST node is entered.
    void
    leavingNode(ASTIterator it, ProgramElement node)
    This method is called just before an AST node is left.
    void
    returnedFromChildNode(ASTIterator it, NonTerminalProgramElement thisNode, ProgramElement childNode)
    Called immediately after the iterator returned from the child node.

  • Field Details

    • ENTER_NONE

      static final int ENTER_NONE
      Return value for ASTListener methods, that signals that no child node should be entered.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • ENTER_SOME

      static final int ENTER_SOME
      Return value for ASTListener methods, that signals that only some children nodes should be entered - each child will be queried separately.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • ENTER_ALL

      static final int ENTER_ALL
      Return value for ASTListener methods, that signals that all children should be entered without further questions.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

  • Method Details

    • enteringNode

      void enteringNode(ASTIterator it, ProgramElement node)
      This method is called whenever an AST node is entered.
      Parameters:
      it - the ASTIterator that called this method.
      node - the node that is currently entered.

    • leavingNode

      void leavingNode(ASTIterator it, ProgramElement node)
      This method is called just before an AST node is left.
      Parameters:
      it - the ASTIterator that called this method.
      node - the node that is about to be left.

    • enterChildren

      int enterChildren(ASTIterator it, NonTerminalProgramElement thisNode)
      Called to determine whether or not children nodes should be visited.
      Parameters:
      it - the ASTIterator that called this method.
      thisNode - the current node.
      Returns:
      either ENTER_NONE, ENTER_SOME or ENTER_ALL.

    • enterChildNode

      boolean enterChildNode(ASTIterator it, NonTerminalProgramElement thisNode, ProgramElement childNode)
      Determines whether or not a given child node should be visited.
      Parameters:
      it - the ASTIterator that called this method.
      thisNode - the current node.
      childNode - the child node that might be visited next.
      Returns:
      true if the iterator should proceed to the given child node.

    • returnedFromChildNode

      void returnedFromChildNode(ASTIterator it, NonTerminalProgramElement thisNode, ProgramElement childNode)
      Called immediately after the iterator returned from the child node.
      Parameters:
      it - the ASTIterator that called this method.
      thisNode - the current node.
      childNode - the child node that has just been visited.
